checks.a_analysis: The HTML output maintains the visual organization of the source PDF. The document is structured by vendor (Broadcom/Symantec, Sophos, Trellix/McAfee, CrowdStrike, Cisco), and within each section, the specific price lists and descriptive fields (customization, training, etc.) are presented in the same order as the source. The transition from the PDF's fixed pages to a continuous HTML flow is handled well, keeping related information together.

All tables from the source PDF, including the complex grids for CrowdStrike (Page 16) and the multi-page price lists for Carahsoft Trellix (Pages 13-15), are rendered as functional HTML tables. The borders are intact, headers are clearly defined, and cell content is properly aligned and readable. Even the more complex layouts, such as the 'Better' and 'Best' tiers for Sophos (Pages 5-9), are correctly translated into distinct table structures in the HTML.

The source PDF is a text-heavy price list and does not contain any meaningful images such as logos, photos, charts, or diagrams. All essential content is text-based and has been captured in the HTML output.

A thorough review of the HTML output confirms that there is no duplicated body content. Each section, table, and paragraph from the source PDF appears exactly once in the converted document's body. The presence of headings in both the Table of Contents and the body is an intentional accessibility feature and does not constitute content duplication.
